task_completion.description: Navigate to or select the 'world_capitals' layer in the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField interface shows the 'world_capitals' layer selected in the layer panel, search interface, or feature list.

task_completion.description: Access the feature attributes for Tokyo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A feature identification or edit screen is open, displaying attributes for the city 'Tokyo'.

task_completion.description: Update the iso_code for Tokyo to 'JP'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'JP' is visible in the 'iso_code' input field on Tokyo's attribute form.

task_completion.description: Access the feature attributes for Canberra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A feature identification or edit screen is open, displaying attributes for the city 'Canberra'.

task_completion.description: Update the iso_code for Canberra to 'AU'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'AU' is visible in the 'iso_code' input field on Canberra's attribute form.

task_completion.description: Access the feature attributes for Brasilia.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A feature identification or edit screen is open, displaying attributes for the city 'Brasilia'.

task_completion.description: Update the iso_code for Brasilia to 'BR'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'BR' is visible in the 'iso_code' input field on Brasilia's attribute form.

integrity.description: The agent used the QField graphical user interface to make the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the QField application interface (map view, feature forms, layer lists) being used, rather than a terminal emulator or command line interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a third-party SQLite database editor to bypass the GIS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Edits are performed within QField's attribute forms, not in an app like 'SQLite Viewer' or 'DB Browser'.

integrity.description: The agent actually searched for or tapped on the specific map features to edit them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows navigation, searching, or map interaction leading to the opening of the specific feature forms for Tokyo, Canberra, and Brasilia.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'world_survey.gpkg' and the target layer is 'world_capitals'. The correct ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes to be entered are: 'JP' for Tokyo, 'AU' for Canberra, and 'BR' for Brasilia. The task must be completed within the QField application environment.
